nameMeanings.name: FitzWilliam
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish (of Norman origin): from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name William .

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth FitzWilliam was born in 1400, in Sprotbrough and Cusworth, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, William Fitzwilliam Of Sprotbrough, was 20 and her mother, Isabel Deincourt, was 20. She married Thomas Congrave in 1418. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died in 1450, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 50.

ko.Sprotborough, Yorkshire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 스프로트브루그h 앤드 커스워스
ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth FitzWilliam(이)가 1400년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 스프로트브루그h 앤드 커스워스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Fitzwilliam Of Sprotbrough(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel Deincourt(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1418년 Thomas Congrave(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1450년 영국 잉글랜드에서 50세의 나이로 사망했다.
